executiveTruth.coreConclusion: The repo has broad launch-safe state coverage, but 0/50 states currently satisfy the stricter modeled-completeness audit and the product is still far from exhaustive across all information types it implies.

informationTypes.label: Public Administrative and Education Routing
subtypes: state DD or IDD routing agencies

nationalGapMatrix.label: Programs, forms, appeals, and waitlists
nationalGapMatrix.evidence: 507 programs, 992 steps, 451 appeals, 165 waitlists across 50 states.
nationalGapMatrix.exhaustiveGap: Waitlist depth is materially shallower than the rest of the program layer.

nationalGapMatrix.label: DD routing, county offices, and education routing
nationalGapMatrix.evidence: 3678 county offices, 636 state routing agencies, 313 regional education agencies, 3117 school districts.
nationalGapMatrix.exhaustiveGap: Routing exists nationally, but regional education depth is still not fully even across all states and counties.

nationalGapMatrix.label: Local providers, clinics, therapists, and care
nationalGapMatrix.evidence: 96 public provider rows and 23 staged provider rows across all 50 states.
nationalGapMatrix.exhaustiveGap: This is the biggest information hole if the goal is all local help, especially to compete on care and treatment discovery.

nationalGapMatrix.label: Nonprofit coverage quality and local utility
nationalGapMatrix.exhaustiveGap: Coverage is broad, but accessibility, capacity, and local in-person truth signals are still sparse.

nationalGapMatrix.label: Advocates and legal/IEP support
nationalGapMatrix.evidence: 2995 advocate rows, with California still blocked in strict gold because 58 counties lose advocate coverage after truth gating.
nationalGapMatrix.exhaustiveGap: Advocate count is decent, but truth-safe local coverage is not yet strong enough to call exhaustive.

nationalGapMatrix.label: Findhelp-like metadata: availability, accessibility, intake, capacity
nationalGapMatrix.evidence: Provider accessibility booleans with true signal: accepts_medi_cal 27/96; interpreter_available 22/96; asl_available 12/96; wheelchair_accessible 5/96; virtual_services 18/96; in_person_services 70/96; home_visits 3/96; transportation_help 9/96 Nonprofit accessibility booleans with true signal: interpreter_available 0/29501; asl_available 0/29501; wheelchair_accessible 0/29501; virtual_services 1/29501; in_person_services 22/29501; home_visits 0/29501; transportation_help 0/29501 Advocate accessibility booleans with true signal: interpreter_available 0/2995; asl_available 0/2995; wheelchair_accessible 0/2995; virtual_services 2/2995; in_person_services 0/2995; home_visits 0/2995; transportation_help 0/2995
nationalGapMatrix.exhaustiveGap: The schema exists, but live high-signal metadata is sparse, especially on nonprofits and advocates.

nationalGapMatrix.label: Canonical org -> program -> location normalization
nationalGapMatrix.evidence: 36902 organizations, 36902 org-program links, 94 service locations, 4314 office locations, 33130 virtual service areas.
nationalGapMatrix.exhaustiveGap: Normalization exists, but service-location depth is still too thin to support a fully location-rich help product.

nationalGapMatrix.label: Conditions, functional needs, age bands, insurance types
nationalGapMatrix.evidence: 78 conditions, 18 functional needs, 7 age bands, 4 insurance types.
nationalGapMatrix.exhaustiveGap: Conditions, needs, age bands, and insurance reference tables are all present, though broader knowledge depth is still driven by the article layer.

nationalGapMatrix.label: Help content and explanatory knowledge
nationalGapMatrix.exhaustiveGap: The content layer is still far too small for the full family journey nationally, but staged knowledge growth is now present and measured.

nationalGapMatrix.label: Family workflow, case tracking, reminders, and documents
nationalGapMatrix.evidence: 5 total family-case-layer rows across 8 tables; most tables are empty.
nationalGapMatrix.exhaustiveGap: The runtime workflow model exists but is not populated as a real product layer.

nationalGapMatrix.label: Support planning, collaboration, and care coordination
nationalGapMatrix.exhaustiveGap: This entire product surface is still schema-only in practice.

nationalGapMatrix.label: User submissions, coverage gaps, verification queue
nationalGapMatrix.evidence: 0 user submissions, 0 coverage gaps, 2 verification queue items.
nationalGapMatrix.exhaustiveGap: Operational feedback loops are not built out enough to sustain exhaustive maintenance.

fullGapLayers.label: Programs, waivers, appeals, forms, and waitlists
fullGapLayers.gap: Program foundation is strong, but waitlist depth is still smaller than the rest of the program layer.

fullGapLayers.label: County offices, DD routing, and education routing
fullGapLayers.gap: Routing layers are broadly populated, but density and local depth still vary by state and county.

fullGapLayers.label: Local nonprofit support organizations
fullGapLayers.gap: Nonprofit coverage is broad, but foundation metadata like accessibility and live capacity is still sparse.

fullGapLayers.label: IEP advocates and advocacy support
fullGapLayers.gap: Advocate count is strong, but truth quality and public eligibility still need to stay under active audit.

fullGapLayers.label: Clinics, therapists, and local providers
evidence: 125 staged provider rows waiting in provider staging
fullGapLayers.gap: Provider coverage is the clearest national information gap. This layer exists, but it is nowhere near all-state depth.

fullGapLayers.label: Findhelp-like metadata on directory listings
evidence: Schema support exists for tags, availability, next steps, accessibility, claims, and trust fields on providers, nonprofits, and advocates.
Those fields sit on 32592 directory rows total.
fullGapLayers.gap: The metadata model exists, but live availability, accessibility, and capacity signals are still sparse on the checked-in DB.

fullGapLayers.label: Organization -> program -> location normalization
fullGapLayers.gap: This migration landing zone is modeled in schema but not yet populated in the checked-in DB.

fullGapLayers.label: Conditions, functional needs, and reference knowledge
fullGapLayers.gap: No major structural gap detected in this layer.

fullGapLayers.label: Knowledge articles and guidance content
fullGapLayers.gap: Knowledge content exists and staged growth is underway, but live article volume is still small relative to the breadth of the rest of the information model.

fullGapLayers.label: Family, case, and navigator-adjacent workflow data
fullGapLayers.gap: The schema supports family/case workflows, but the checked-in DB mostly shows demo-level or empty runtime data.

fullGapLayers.label: User submissions, coverage gaps, and feedback loops
fullGapLayers.gap: These operational loops are modeled but not populated in the checked-in DB snapshot.

immediateTruths: 50/50 modeled completeness does not mean all information categories are deeply built out.
0/50 strict-gold states means 1 state still sits in the public-safe-but-blocked lane on the stricter truth registry.
Provider coverage and knowledge-content depth are still the largest visible product information gaps.
Directory metadata exists in schema, but most nonprofit and advocate rows still lack rich accessibility and capacity signals.
Several workflow and support layers are still mostly empty despite having schema support.
